[12] ==== SURROGATE {Performer} -> "Data Analysis Code"  =============================================================================================
Here is the code to perform the analysis.
It creates the files: ['table_0.pkl', 'table_1.pkl', 'table_2.pkl', 'additional_results.pkl'].
# IMPORT
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import pickle
from statsmodels.formula.api import ols
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler

# LOAD DATA
data_infection = pd.read_csv('TimeToInfection.csv')
data_symptoms = pd.read_csv('Symptoms.csv')

# DATASET PREPARATIONS
# Merge the two dataframes based on 'ID' and 'group'
data_all = pd.merge(data_infection, data_symptoms, on=['ID', 'group'])

# Convert 'positive_household' to binary and create new column
data_all['positive_household_binary'] = data_all['positive_household'].map({'yes': 1, 'no': 0})

# DESCRIPTIVE STATISTICS
## Table 0: "Descriptive statistics of infection events, mean symptoms & mean time since last infection per group"
event_counts = data_all.groupby('group')['infection_event'].sum()
mean_symptoms = data_all.groupby('group')['symptom_number'].mean()
mean_time_since_imm = data_all.groupby('group')['months_since_immunisation'].mean()

df0 = pd.DataFrame({'InfectionEvents': event_counts, 'MeanSymptoms': mean_symptoms, 'MeanMonthsSinceImm': mean_time_since_imm})

# Replacing NaN values with "NA" for Group N
df0.fillna("NA", inplace=True)

df0.to_pickle('table_0.pkl')

# PREPROCESSING
# Creating dummy variables for categorical variables
data_all = pd.get_dummies(data_all, columns=['group', 'sex_x', 'BMI_x', 'vaccinetype', 'variant'])

# Standardization
scaler = StandardScaler()
scaled_data = scaler.fit_transform(data_all[['age_x', 'months_since_immunisation', 'time_dose1_to_dose_2']])
scaled_df = pd.DataFrame(scaled_data, index=data_all.index, columns=['scaled_age', 'scaled_months_since_immunisation', 'scaled_time_dose1_to_dose_2'])
data_all = pd.concat([data_all, scaled_df], axis=1)

# ANALYSIS
## Table 1: "Association between months_since_immunisation and symptom_number"
model1 = ols('symptom_number ~ scaled_months_since_immunisation', data=data_all).fit()
df1 = model1.summary2().tables[1]
df1.to_pickle('table_1.pkl')

## Table 2: "Association between patient_contact and symptom_number, accounting for mask usage and household infections"
model2 = ols('symptom_number ~ patient_contact + using_FFP2_mask_x + positive_household_binary', data=data_all).fit()
df2 = model2.summary2().tables[1]
df2.to_pickle('table_2.pkl')

# SAVE ADDITIONAL RESULTS
additional_results = {
    'Total number of observations': len(data_all),
    'Number of unique health workers': data_all['ID'].nunique(),
}

with open('additional_results.pkl', 'wb') as f:
    pickle.dump(additional_results, f)
======================================================================================================================================================

AppendMessage, Adding the debugged code as if it was the original response.
[12] ==== SURROGATE {Performer} -> "Data Analysis Code"  =============================================================================================
Here is the code to perform the analysis.
It creates the files: ['table_0.pkl', 'table_1.pkl', 'table_2.pkl', 'additional_results.pkl'].
# IMPORT
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import pickle
from statsmodels.formula.api import ols
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler

# LOAD DATA
data_infection = pd.read_csv('TimeToInfection.csv')
data_symptoms = pd.read_csv('Symptoms.csv')

# DATASET PREPARATIONS
# Merge the two dataframes based on 'ID' and 'group'
data_all = pd.merge(data_infection, data_symptoms, on=['ID', 'group'])

# Gather only the records with infection_event == 1, and remove duplicate entries based on the 'ID' column
data_all_infected = data_all[data_all['infection_event'] == 1].drop_duplicates(subset='ID')

# Convert 'positive_household' to binary and create new column
data_all_infected['positive_household_binary'] = data_all_infected['positive_household'].map({'yes': 1, 'no': 0})

# DESCRIPTIVE STATISTICS
## Table 0: "Descriptive statistics of infection events, mean symptoms & mean time since last infection per group"
event_counts = data_all.groupby('group')['infection_event'].sum()
mean_symptoms = data_all_infected.groupby('group')['symptom_number'].mean()
mean_time_since_imm = data_all_infected.groupby('group')['months_since_immunisation'].mean()

df0 = pd.DataFrame({'InfectionEvents': event_counts, 'MeanSymptoms': mean_symptoms, 'MeanMonthsSinceImm': mean_time_since_imm})

# Replacing NaN values with "NA" for Group N
df0.fillna("NA", inplace=True)

df0.to_pickle('table_0.pkl')

# PREPROCESSING
# Creating dummy variables for categorical variables
data_all_infected = pd.get_dummies(data_all_infected, columns=['group', 'sex_x', 'BMI_x', 'vaccinetype', 'variant'])

# Standardization
scaler = StandardScaler()
scaled_data = scaler.fit_transform(data_all_infected[['age_x', 'months_since_immunisation', 'time_dose1_to_dose_2']])
scaled_df = pd.DataFrame(scaled_data, index=data_all_infected.index, columns=['scaled_age', 'scaled_months_since_immunisation',
'scaled_time_dose1_to_dose_2'])
data_all_infected = pd.concat([data_all_infected, scaled_df], axis=1)

# ANALYSIS
## Table 1: "Association between months_since_immunisation and symptom_number"
model1 = ols('symptom_number ~ scaled_months_since_immunisation', data=data_all_infected).fit()
df1 = model1.summary2().tables[1]
df1.to_pickle('table_1.pkl')

## Table 2: "Association between patient_contact and symptom_number, accounting for mask usage and household infections"
model2 = ols('symptom_number ~ patient_contact + using_FFP2_mask_x + positive_household_binary', data=data_all_infected).fit()
df2 = model2.summary2().tables[1]
df2.to_pickle('table_2.pkl')

# SAVE ADDITIONAL RESULTS
additional_results = {
    'Total number of observations': len(data_all),
    'Number of unique health workers': data_all['ID'].nunique(),
}

with open('additional_results.pkl', 'wb') as f:
    pickle.dump(additional_results, f)
======================================================================================================================================================

[12] ==== SURROGATE {Performer} -> "Data Analysis Code"  =============================================================================================
Here is the code to perform the analysis.
It creates the files: ['table_0.pkl', 'table_1.pkl', 'table_2.pkl', 'additional_results.pkl'].
# IMPORT
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import pickle
from statsmodels.formula.api import ols
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler

# LOAD DATA
data_infection = pd.read_csv('TimeToInfection.csv')
data_symptoms = pd.read_csv('Symptoms.csv')

# DATASET PREPARATIONS
# Merge the two dataframes based on 'ID' and 'group'
data_all = pd.merge(data_infection, data_symptoms, on=['ID', 'group'])

# Gather only the records with infection_event == 1 and remove duplicate entries based on the 'ID' column
data_all_infected = data_all[data_all['infection_event'] == 1].drop_duplicates(subset='ID')

# Convert 'positive_household' to binary and create new column
data_all_infected['positive_household_binary'] = data_all_infected['positive_household'].map({'yes': 1, 'no': 0})

# DESCRIPTIVE STATISTICS
## Table 0: "Descriptive statistics of infection events, mean symptoms & mean time since last infection per group"
event_counts = data_all.groupby('group')['infection_event'].sum()
mean_symptoms = data_all_infected.groupby('group')['symptom_number'].mean()
mean_time_since_imm = data_all_infected.groupby('group')['months_since_immunisation'].mean()

df0 = pd.DataFrame({'InfectionEvents': event_counts, 'MeanSymptoms': mean_symptoms, 'MeanMonthsSinceImm': mean_time_since_imm})

# Replacing NaN values with "NA" for Group N
df0.fillna("NA", inplace=True)

df0.to_pickle('table_0.pkl')

# PREPROCESSING
# Creating dummy variables for categorical variables.
data_all_infected = pd.get_dummies(data_all_infected, columns=['group', 'sex_x', 'BMI_x', 'vaccinetype', 'variant'])

# Standardization
scaler = StandardScaler()
scaled_data = scaler.fit_transform(data_all_infected[['age_x', 'months_since_immunisation', 'time_dose1_to_dose_2']])
scaled_df = pd.DataFrame(scaled_data, index=data_all_infected.index, columns=['scaled_age', 'scaled_months_since_immunisation',
'scaled_time_dose1_to_dose_2'])
data_all_infected = pd.concat([data_all_infected, scaled_df], axis=1)

# ANALYSIS
## Table 1: "Association between months_since_immunisation and symptom_number"
model1 = ols('symptom_number ~ scaled_months_since_immunisation', data=data_all_infected).fit()
df1 = model1.summary2().tables[1]
df1.to_pickle('table_1.pkl')

## Table 2: "Association between patient_contact and symptom_number, accounting for mask usage and household infections"
model2 = ols('symptom_number ~ patient_contact + using_FFP2_mask_x + positive_household_binary', data=data_all_infected).fit()
df2 = model2.summary2().tables[1]
df2.to_pickle('table_2.pkl')

# SAVE ADDITIONAL RESULTS
additional_results = {
    'Total number of health workers': data_all['ID'].nunique(),
    'Number of infected health workers': data_all_infected['ID'].nunique(),
}

with open('additional_results.pkl', 'wb') as f:
    pickle.dump(additional_results, f)
======================================================================================================================================================

```output
Intercept                           3.727   0.08255   45.15  <1e-06   3.565   3.889
scaled_months_since_immunisation -0.07805   0.08255 -0.9454   0.345 -0.2401 0.08404
```

"table_2.pkl":
```output
                             Coef.  Std.Err.       t   P>|t|  [0.025  0.975]
Intercept                    3.838    0.1933   19.86  <1e-06   3.458   4.217
patient_contact           -0.02841    0.1996 -0.1423   0.887 -0.4203  0.3635
using_FFP2_mask_x          0.01422     0.214 0.06644   0.947 -0.4059  0.4343
positive_household_binary -0.01725    0.1611 -0.1071   0.915 -0.3336  0.2991
```

[12] ==== SURROGATE {Performer} -> "Data Analysis Code"  =============================================================================================
Here is the code to perform the analysis.
It creates the files: ['table_0.pkl', 'table_1.pkl', 'table_2.pkl', 'additional_results.pkl'].
# IMPORT
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import pickle
from statsmodels.formula.api import ols
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler

# LOAD DATA
data_infection = pd.read_csv('TimeToInfection.csv')
data_symptoms = pd.read_csv('Symptoms.csv')

# DATASET PREPARATIONS
# Merge the two dataframes based on 'ID' and 'group'
data_all = pd.merge(data_infection, data_symptoms, on=['ID', 'group'])

# Gather only the records with infection_event == 1 and remove duplicate entries based on the 'ID' column
data_all_infected = data_all[data_all['infection_event'] == 1].drop_duplicates(subset='ID')

# Convert 'positive_household' to binary and create a new column
data_all_infected['positive_household_binary'] = data_all_infected['positive_household'].map({'yes': 1, 'no': 0})

# DESCRIPTIVE STATISTICS
## Table 0: "Descriptive statistics of infection events, mean symptoms & mean time since last infection per group"
event_counts = data_all.groupby('group')['infection_event'].sum()
mean_symptoms = data_all_infected.groupby('group')['symptom_number'].mean()
mean_time_since_imm = data_all_infected.groupby('group')['months_since_immunisation'].mean()

df0 = pd.DataFrame({'InfectionEvents': event_counts, 'MeanSymptoms': mean_symptoms, 'MeanMonthsSinceImm': mean_time_since_imm})

# Replacing NaN values with "NA" for Group N
df0.fillna("NA", inplace=True)

df0.to_pickle('table_0.pkl')

# PREPROCESSING
# Creating dummy variables for categorical variables.
data_all_infected = pd.get_dummies(data_all_infected, columns=['group', 'sex_x', 'BMI_x', 'vaccinetype', 'variant'])

# Standardization
scaler = StandardScaler()
scaled_data = scaler.fit_transform(data_all_infected[['age_x', 'months_since_immunisation', 'time_dose1_to_dose_2']])
scaled_df = pd.DataFrame(scaled_data, index=data_all_infected.index, columns=['scaled_age', 'scaled_months_since_immunisation',
'scaled_time_dose1_to_dose_2'])
data_all_infected = pd.concat([data_all_infected, scaled_df], axis=1)

# ANALYSIS
## Table 1: "Association between months_since_immunisation and symptom_number"
model1 = ols('symptom_number ~ scaled_months_since_immunisation', data=data_all_infected).fit()
df1 = model1.summary2().tables[1]
df1.to_pickle('table_1.pkl')

## Table 2: "Association between patient_contact and symptom_number, accounting for mask usage and household infections"
model2 = ols('symptom_number ~ patient_contact + using_FFP2_mask_x + positive_household_binary', data=data_all_infected).fit()
df2 = model2.summary2().tables[1]
df2.to_pickle('table_2.pkl')

# SAVE ADDITIONAL RESULTS
additional_results = {
    'Total number of observations': len(data_all),
    'Number of infected health workers': len(data_all_infected),
}

with open('additional_results.pkl', 'wb') as f:
    pickle.dump(additional_results, f)
======================================================================================================================================================

[12] ==== SURROGATE {Performer} -> "Data Analysis Code"  =============================================================================================
Here is the code to perform the analysis.
It creates the files: ['table_0.pkl', 'table_1.pkl', 'table_2.pkl', 'additional_results.pkl'].
# IMPORT
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import pickle
from statsmodels.formula.api import ols
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler

# LOAD DATA
data_infection = pd.read_csv('TimeToInfection.csv')
data_symptoms = pd.read_csv('Symptoms.csv')

# DATASET PREPARATIONS
# Merge the two dataframes based on 'ID' and 'group'
data_all = pd.merge(data_infection, data_symptoms, on=['ID', 'group'])

# Gather only the records with infection_event == 1 and remove duplicate entries based on the 'ID' column
data_all_infected = data_all[data_all['infection_event'] == 1].drop_duplicates(subset='ID')

# Convert 'positive_household' to binary and create a new column
data_all_infected['positive_household_binary'] = data_all_infected['positive_household'].map({'yes': 1, 'no': 0})

# Exclude the data from Group N after performing descriptive analysis
data_all_infected_without_n = data_all_infected[data_all_infected['group'] != 'N']

# DESCRIPTIVE STATISTICS
## Table 0: "Descriptive statistics of infection events, mean symptoms & mean time since last infection per group"
event_counts = data_all.groupby('group')['infection_event'].sum()
mean_symptoms = data_all_infected.groupby('group')['symptom_number'].mean()
mean_time_since_imm = data_all_infected.groupby('group')['months_since_immunisation'].mean()

df0 = pd.DataFrame({'InfectionEvents': event_counts, 'MeanSymptoms': mean_symptoms, 'MeanMonthsSinceImm': mean_time_since_imm})

# Replacing NaN values with "NA" for Group N
df0.fillna("NA", inplace=True)

df0.to_pickle('table_0.pkl')

# PREPROCESSING
# Creating dummy variables for categorical variables.
data_all_infected_without_n = pd.get_dummies(data_all_infected_without_n, columns=['group', 'sex_x', 'BMI_x', 'vaccinetype', 'variant'])

# Standardization
scaler = StandardScaler()
scaled_data = scaler.fit_transform(data_all_infected_without_n[['age_x', 'months_since_immunisation', 'time_dose1_to_dose_2']])
scaled_df = pd.DataFrame(scaled_data, index=data_all_infected_without_n.index, columns=['scaled_age', 'scaled_months_since_immunisation',
'scaled_time_dose1_to_dose_2'])
data_all_infected_without_n = pd.concat([data_all_infected_without_n, scaled_df], axis=1)

# ANALYSIS
## Table 1: "Association between months_since_immunisation and symptom_number"
model1 = ols('symptom_number ~ scaled_months_since_immunisation', data=data_all_infected_without_n).fit()
df1 = model1.summary2().tables[1]
df1.to_pickle('table_1.pkl')

## Table 2: "Association between patient_contact and symptom_number, accounting for mask usage and household infections"
model2 = ols('symptom_number ~ patient_contact + using_FFP2_mask_x + positive_household_binary', data=data_all_infected_without_n).fit()
df2 = model2.summary2().tables[1]
df2.to_pickle('table_2.pkl')

# SAVE ADDITIONAL RESULTS
additional_results = {
    'Total number of observations': len(data_all),
    'Number of infected health workers': len(data_all_infected_without_n),
}

with open('additional_results.pkl', 'wb') as f:
    pickle.dump(additional_results, f)
======================================================================================================================================================

[13] ---- USER {Debugger} -> "Data Analysis Code"  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
## Request Review of all created files
I ran your code.

Here is the content of the file(s) that the code created for our scientific paper:

"table_0.pkl":
```output
       InfectionEvents  MeanSymptoms MeanMonthsSinceImm
group
H                  110         3.109              3.875
I                   37         4.081              8.387
N                   67         4.537                 NA
V                  550         3.838              4.985
```

"table_1.pkl":
```output
                                    Coef.  Std.Err.       t   P>|t|  [0.025  0.975]
Intercept                           3.727   0.08255   45.15  <1e-06   3.565   3.889
scaled_months_since_immunisation -0.07805   0.08255 -0.9454   0.345 -0.2401 0.08404
```

"table_2.pkl":

Here is the revised code keeping your suggestions in mind. It creates the files: ['table_0.pkl', 'table_1.pkl', 'table_2.pkl',
'additional_results.pkl']

# IMPORT
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import pickle
from statsmodels.formula.api import ols
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler

# LOAD DATA
data_infection = pd.read_csv('TimeToInfection.csv')
data_symptoms = pd.read_csv('Symptoms.csv')

# DATASET PREPARATIONS
# Merge the two dataframes based on 'ID' and 'group'
data_all = pd.merge(data_infection, data_symptoms, on=['ID', 'group'])
data_infected = data_all[data_all['infection_event'] == 1].drop_duplicates(subset='ID')

# Convert 'positive_household' to binary
data_infected['positive_household_binary'] = data_infected['positive_household'].map({'yes': 1, 'no': 0})

# Exclude unimmunised group
data_infected_immunised = data_infected[data_infected['group'] != 'N']

# DESCRIPTIVE STATISTICS
## Table 0: "Descriptive statistics of infection events, mean symptoms & mean time since last infection per group"
summary_infection_events = data_all.groupby('group')['infection_event'].sum()
summary_avg_symptoms = data_infected.groupby('group')['symptom_number'].mean()
summary_avg_time_since_immunisation = data_infected.groupby('group')['months_since_immunisation'].mean()

df0 = pd.DataFrame({'InfectionEvents': summary_infection_events,
                    'MeanSymptoms': summary_avg_symptoms,
                    'MeanMonthsSinceImmunisation': summary_avg_time_since_immunisation})

# Replacing NaN values with "NA" for group N
df0.fillna("NA", inplace=True)
df0.to_pickle('table_0.pkl')

# PREPROCESSING
# Data Standardization
scaler = StandardScaler()
scaled_data = scaler.fit_transform(data_infected_immunised[['age_x', 'months_since_immunisation', 'time_dose1_to_dose_2']])
scaled_df = pd.DataFrame(scaled_data, index=data_infected_immunised.index, columns=['scaled_age',
                                                                                   'scaled_months_since_immunisation',
                                                                                   'scaled_time_dose1_to_dose_2'])
data_infected_immunised = pd.concat([data_infected_immunised, scaled_df], axis=1)

# Creating dummy variables for categorical variables.
data_infected_immunised = pd.get_dummies(data_infected_immunised, columns=['group', 'sex_x', 'BMI_x', 'vaccinetype', 'variant'])

# ANALYSIS
## Table 1: "Association between months_since_immunisation and symptom_number"
model1 = ols('symptom_number ~ scaled_months_since_immunisation', data=data_infected_immunised).fit()
table1 = model1.summary2().tables[1]
table1.to_pickle('table_1.pkl')

## Table 2: "Association between patient_contact & symptom_number, adjusted for mask usage & household infections"
model2 = ols('symptom_number ~ patient_contact + using_FFP2_mask_x + positive_household_binary', data=data_infected_immunised).fit()
table2 = model2.summary2().tables[1]
table2.to_pickle('table_2.pkl')

# SAVE ADDITIONAL RESULTS
additional_results = {
    'Total number of observations': len(data_all),
    'Number of immunised and infected health workers': len(data_infected_immunised),
}

with open('additional_results.pkl', 'wb') as f:
    pickle.dump(additional_results, f)

Here is the code to perform the analysis.
It creates the files: ['table_0.pkl', 'table_1.pkl', 'table_2.pkl', 'additional_results.pkl'].
# IMPORT
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import pickle
from statsmodels.formula.api import ols
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler

# LOAD DATA
data_infection = pd.read_csv('TimeToInfection.csv')
data_symptoms = pd.read_csv('Symptoms.csv')

# DATASET PREPARATIONS
# Merge the two dataframes based on 'ID' and 'group'
data_all = pd.merge(data_infection, data_symptoms, on=['ID', 'group'])
data_infected = data_all[data_all['infection_event'] == 1].drop_duplicates(subset='ID')

# Convert 'positive_household' to binary
data_infected['positive_household_binary'] = data_infected['positive_household'].map({'yes': 1, 'no': 0})

# Exclude unimmunised group
data_infected_immunised = data_infected[data_infected['group'] != 'N']

# DESCRIPTIVE STATISTICS
## Table 0: "Descriptive statistics of infection events, mean symptoms & mean time since last infection per group"
summary_infection_events = data_all.groupby('group')['infection_event'].sum()
summary_avg_symptoms = data_infected.groupby('group')['symptom_number'].mean()
summary_avg_time_since_immunisation = data_infected.groupby('group')['months_since_immunisation'].mean()

df0 = pd.DataFrame({'InfectionEvents': summary_infection_events,
                    'MeanSymptoms': summary_avg_symptoms,
                    'MeanMonthsSinceImmunisation': summary_avg_time_since_immunisation})

# Replacing NaN values with "NA" for group N
df0.fillna("NA", inplace=True)
df0.to_pickle('table_0.pkl')

# PREPROCESSING
# Data Standardization
scaler = StandardScaler()
scaled_data = scaler.fit_transform(data_infected_immunised[['age_x', 'months_since_immunisation', 'time_dose1_to_dose_2']])
scaled_df = pd.DataFrame(scaled_data, index=data_infected_immunised.index, columns=['scaled_age',
                                                                                   'scaled_months_since_immunisation',
                                                                                   'scaled_time_dose1_to_dose_2'])
data_infected_immunised = pd.concat([data_infected_immunised, scaled_df], axis=1)

# Creating dummy variables for categorical variables.
data_infected_immunised = pd.get_dummies(data_infected_immunised, columns=['group', 'sex_x', 'BMI_x', 'vaccinetype', 'variant'])

# ANALYSIS
## Table 1: "Association between months_since_immunisation and symptom_number"
model1 = ols('symptom_number ~ scaled_months_since_immunisation', data=data_infected_immunised).fit()
table1 = model1.summary2().tables[1]
table1.to_pickle('table_1.pkl')

## Table 2: "Association between patient_contact & symptom_number, adjusted for mask usage & household infections"
model2 = ols('symptom_number ~ patient_contact + using_FFP2_mask_x + positive_household_binary', data=data_infected_immunised).fit()
table2 = model2.summary2().tables[1]
table2.to_pickle('table_2.pkl')

# SAVE ADDITIONAL RESULTS
additional_results = {
    'Total number of observations': len(data_all),
    'Number of immunised and infected health workers': len(data_infected_immunised),
}

with open('additional_results.pkl', 'wb') as f:
    pickle.dump(additional_results, f)

[15] ==== SURROGATE {Performer} -> "Data Analysis Code"  =============================================================================================
Here is the code to perform the requested analysis:
# IMPORT
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import pickle
from statsmodels.formula.api import ols
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler

# LOAD DATA
data_infection = pd.read_csv('TimeToInfection.csv')
data_symptoms = pd.read_csv('Symptoms.csv')

# DATASET PREPARATIONS
# Merge the two dataframes based on 'ID' and 'group'
data_all = pd.merge(data_infection, data_symptoms, on=['ID', 'group'])
data_infected = data_all[data_all['infection_event'] == 1].drop_duplicates(subset='ID')

# Convert 'positive_household' to binary
data_infected['positive_household_binary'] = data_infected['positive_household'].map({'yes': 1, 'no': 0})

# Exclude unimmunised group
data_infected_immunised = data_infected[data_infected['group'] != 'N']

# DESCRIPTIVE STATISTICS
## Table 0: "Descriptive statistics of infection events, mean symptoms & mean time since last infection per group"
summary_infection_events = data_all.groupby('group')['infection_event'].sum()
summary_avg_symptoms = data_infected.groupby('group')['symptom_number'].mean()
summary_avg_time_since_immunisation = data_infected.groupby('group')['months_since_immunisation'].mean()

df0 = pd.DataFrame({'InfectionEvents': summary_infection_events,
                    'MeanSymptoms': summary_avg_symptoms,
                    'MeanMonthsSinceImmunisation': summary_avg_time_since_immunisation})

# Replacing NaN values with "NA" for group N
df0.fillna("NA", inplace=True)
df0.to_pickle('table_0.pkl')

# PREPROCESSING
# Data Standardization
scaler = StandardScaler()
scaled_data = scaler.fit_transform(data_infected_immunised[['age_x', 'months_since_immunisation', 'time_dose1_to_dose_2']])
scaled_df = pd.DataFrame(scaled_data, index=data_infected_immunised.index, columns=['scaled_age',
                                                                                   'scaled_months_since_immunisation',
                                                                                   'scaled_time_dose1_to_dose_2'])
data_infected_immunised = pd.concat([data_infected_immunised, scaled_df], axis=1)

# Creating dummy variables for categorical variables.
data_infected_immunised = pd.get_dummies(data_infected_immunised, columns=['group', 'sex_x', 'BMI_x', 'vaccinetype', 'variant'])

# ANALYSIS
## Table 1: "Association between months_since_immunisation, group, interaction between group and months_since_immunisation, and symptom_number"
model1 = ols('symptom_number ~ scaled_months_since_immunisation + group_V + group_I + group_H + group_V:scaled_months_since_immunisation +
group_I:scaled_months_since_immunisation + group_H:scaled_months_since_immunisation', data=data_infected_immunised).fit()
table1 = model1.summary2().tables[1]
table1.to_pickle('table_1.pkl')

## Table 2: "Association between patient_contact & symptom_number, adjusted for mask usage & household infections"
model2 = ols('symptom_number ~ patient_contact + using_FFP2_mask_x + positive_household_binary', data=data_infected_immunised).fit()
table2 = model2.summary2().tables[1]
table2.to_pickle('table_2.pkl')

# SAVE ADDITIONAL RESULTS
additional_results = {
    'Total number of observations': len(data_all),
    'Number of immunised and infected health workers': len(data_infected_immunised),
}

with open('additional_results.pkl', 'wb') as f:

[15] ==== SURROGATE {Performer} -> "Data Analysis Code"  =============================================================================================
Here is the code to perform the requested analysis:
# IMPORT
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import pickle
from statsmodels.formula.api import ols
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler

# LOAD DATA
data_infection = pd.read_csv('TimeToInfection.csv')
data_symptoms = pd.read_csv('Symptoms.csv')

# DATASET PREPARATIONS
# Merge the two dataframes based on 'ID' and 'group'
data_all = pd.merge(data_infection, data_symptoms, on=['ID', 'group'])
data_infected = data_all[data_all['infection_event'] == 1].drop_duplicates(subset='ID')

# Convert 'positive_household' to binary
data_infected['positive_household_binary'] = data_infected['positive_household'].map({'yes': 1, 'no': 0})

# Exclude unimmunised group
data_infected_immunised = data_infected[data_infected['group'] != 'N']

# DESCRIPTIVE STATISTICS
## Table 0: "Descriptive statistics of infection events, mean symptoms & mean time since last infection per group"
summary_infection_events = data_all.groupby('group')['infection_event'].sum()
summary_avg_symptoms = data_infected.groupby('group')['symptom_number'].mean()
summary_avg_time_since_immunisation = data_infected.groupby('group')['months_since_immunisation'].mean()

df0 = pd.DataFrame({'InfectionEvents': summary_infection_events,
                    'MeanSymptoms': summary_avg_symptoms,
                    'MeanMonthsSinceImmunisation': summary_avg_time_since_immunisation})

# Replacing NaN values with "NA" for group N
df0.fillna("NA", inplace=True)
df0.to_pickle('table_0.pkl')

# PREPROCESSING
# Data Standardization
scaler = StandardScaler()
scaled_data = scaler.fit_transform(data_infected_immunised[['age_x', 'months_since_immunisation']])
scaled_df = pd.DataFrame(scaled_data, index=data_infected_immunised.index, columns=['scaled_age',
                                                                                   'scaled_months_since_immunisation'])
data_infected_immunised = pd.concat([data_infected_immunised, scaled_df], axis=1)

# Creating dummy variables for categorical variables
data_infected_immunised = pd.get_dummies(data_infected_immunised, columns=['group', 'sex_x', 'BMI_x', 'vaccinetype', 'variant'])

# Removing unused columns to avoid multicollinearity
data_infected_immunised.drop(['group_N', 'sex_x_', 'BMI_x_', 'vaccinetype_'], axis=1, inplace=True)
# ANALYSIS
## Table 1: "Association between months_since_immunisation and symptom_number"
model1 = ols('symptom_number ~ scaled_months_since_immunisation + group_V + group_I + group_H', data=data_infected_immunised).fit()
table1 = model1.summary2().tables[1]
table1.to_pickle('table_1.pkl')

## Table 2: "Association between patient_contact & symptom_number, adjusted for mask usage & household infections"
model2 = ols('symptom_number ~ patient_contact + using_FFP2_mask_x + positive_household_binary', data=data_infected_immunised).fit()
table2 = model2.summary2().tables[1]
table2.to_pickle('table_2.pkl')

# SAVE ADDITIONAL RESULTS
additional_results = {
    'Total number of observations': len(data_all),
    'Number of immunised and infected health workers': len(data_infected_immunised),
}

with open('additional_results.pkl', 'wb') as f:
    pickle.dump(additional_results, f)
======================================================================================================================================================

[15] ==== SURROGATE {Performer} -> "Data Analysis Code"  =============================================================================================
Here is the code to perform the requested analysis:
# IMPORT
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import pickle
from statsmodels.formula.api import ols
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler

# LOAD DATA
data_infection = pd.read_csv('TimeToInfection.csv')
data_symptoms = pd.read_csv('Symptoms.csv')

# DATASET PREPARATIONS
# Merge the two dataframes based on 'ID' and 'group'
data_all = pd.merge(data_infection, data_symptoms, on=['ID', 'group'])
data_infected = data_all[data_all['infection_event'] == 1].drop_duplicates(subset='ID')

# Convert 'positive_household' to binary
data_infected['positive_household_binary'] = data_infected['positive_household'].map({'yes': 1, 'no': 0})

# Exclude unimmunised group
data_infected_immunised = data_infected[data_infected['group'] != 'N']

# DESCRIPTIVE STATISTICS
## Table 0: "Descriptive statistics of infection events, mean symptoms & mean time since last infection per group"
summary_infection_events = data_all.groupby('group')['infection_event'].sum()
summary_avg_symptoms = data_infected.groupby('group')['symptom_number'].mean()
summary_avg_time_since_immunisation = data_infected.groupby('group')['months_since_immunisation'].mean()

df0 = pd.DataFrame({'InfectionEvents': summary_infection_events,
                    'MeanSymptoms': summary_avg_symptoms,
                    'MeanMonthsSinceImmunisation': summary_avg_time_since_immunisation})

# Replace NaN values with "NA" for group N
df0.fillna("NA", inplace=True)
df0.to_pickle('table_0.pkl')

# PREPROCESSING
# Data Standardization
scaler = StandardScaler()
scaled_data = scaler.fit_transform(data_infected_immunised[['age_x', 'months_since_immunisation']])
scaled_df = pd.DataFrame(scaled_data, index=data_infected_immunised.index, columns=['scaled_age',
                                                                                   'scaled_months_since_immunisation'])
data_infected_immunised = pd.concat([data_infected_immunised, scaled_df], axis=1)

# Creating dummy variables for categorical variables
data_infected_immunised = pd.get_dummies(data_infected_immunised)
# ANALYSIS
## Table 1: "Association between months_since_immunisation and symptom_number"
model1 = ols('symptom_number ~ scaled_months_since_immunisation + group_V + group_I + group_H', data=data_infected_immunised).fit()
table1 = model1.summary2().tables[1]
table1.to_pickle('table_1.pkl')

## Table 2: "Association between patient_contact & symptom_number, adjusted for mask usage & household infections"
model2 = ols('symptom_number ~ patient_contact + using_FFP2_mask_x + positive_household_binary', data=data_infected_immunised).fit()
table2 = model2.summary2().tables[1]
table2.to_pickle('table_2.pkl')

# SAVE ADDITIONAL RESULTS
additional_results = {
    'Total number of observations': len(data_all),
    'Number of immunised and infected health workers': len(data_infected_immunised),
}

with open('additional_results.pkl', 'wb') as f:
    pickle.dump(additional_results, f)

# IMPORT
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import pickle
from statsmodels.formula.api import ols
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler

# LOAD DATA
data_infection = pd.read_csv('TimeToInfection.csv')
data_symptoms = pd.read_csv('Symptoms.csv')

# DATASET PREPARATIONS
# Merge the two dataframes based on 'ID' and 'group'
data_all = pd.merge(data_infection, data_symptoms, on=['ID', 'group'])
data_infected = data_all[data_all['infection_event'] == 1].drop_duplicates(subset='ID')

# Convert 'positive_household' to binary
data_infected['positive_household_binary'] = data_infected['positive_household'].map({'yes': 1, 'no': 0})

# Exclude unimmunised group
data_infected_immunised = data_infected[data_infected['group'] != 'N']

# DESCRIPTIVE STATISTICS
## Table 0: "Descriptive statistics of infection events, mean symptoms & mean time since last infection per group"
summary_infection_events = data_all.groupby('group')['infection_event'].sum()
summary_avg_symptoms = data_infected.groupby('group')['symptom_number'].mean()
summary_avg_time_since_immunisation = data_infected.groupby('group')['months_since_immunisation'].mean()

df0 = pd.DataFrame({'InfectionEvents': summary_infection_events,
                    'MeanSymptoms': summary_avg_symptoms,
                    'MeanMonthsSinceImmunisation': summary_avg_time_since_immunisation})

# Replacing NaN values with "NA" for group N
df0.fillna("NA", inplace=True)
df0.to_pickle('table_0.pkl')

# PREPROCESSING
# Data Standardization
scaler = StandardScaler()
scaled_data = scaler.fit_transform(data_infected_immunised[['age_x', 'months_since_immunisation']])
scaled_df = pd.DataFrame(scaled_data, index=data_infected_immunised.index, columns=['scaled_age',
                                                                                   'scaled_months_since_immunisation'])
data_infected_immunised = pd.concat([data_infected_immunised, scaled_df], axis=1)

# Creating dummy variables for categorical variables
data_infected_immunised = pd.get_dummies(data_infected_immunised, drop_first=True)

# ANALYSIS
## Table 1: "Association between months_since_immunisation and symptom_number"
model1 = ols('symptom_number ~ scaled_months_since_immunisation + group_V + group_I', data=data_infected_immunised).fit()
table1 = model1.summary2().tables[1]
table1.to_pickle('table_1.pkl')

## Table 2: "Association between patient_contact & symptom_number, adjusted for mask usage & household infections"
model2 = ols('symptom_number ~ patient_contact + using_FFP2_mask_x + positive_household_binary', data=data_infected_immunised).fit()
table2 = model2.summary2().tables[1]
table2.to_pickle('table_2.pkl')

# SAVE ADDITIONAL RESULTS
additional_results = {
    'Total number of observations': len(data_all),
    'Number of immunised and infected health workers': len(data_infected_immunised),
}

with open('additional_results.pkl', 'wb') as f:
    pickle.dump(additional_results, f)

either your concern ("CONCERN", "<your concern>") or your assertion that this check is ok ("OK", "<your assertion>")..:

    # * COMPLETENESS OF TABLES:

}

(1) Generalize.
The points above are just examples, provided as a basis from which you can generalize to checks relevant to our specific code.
(2) Skip irrelevant points.
If any of the points above are not applicable, you can skip them.
(3) Add more checks.
You can also add as many other relevant checks, both positive ("OK", "...") and negative ("CONCERN", "...").
(4) Be specific.
Be specific in the description of the checks (keys) and assessment (values), so that it is clear what you are referring to in the code.

    # Interpretation of Results:

    "Interpretation of results": ("OK", "The results in the tables are interpretable and align with the Hypothesis Testing Plan."),

    # Additional Results:
    # The additional results provide useful information that is not included in the tables.
    "Additional results": ("OK", "The additional results provide useful information that is not included in the tables."),

    # Data Preprocessing:
    # The data preprocessing steps are appropriate and necessary for the analysis.
    "Data preprocessing": ("OK", "The data preprocessing steps are appropriate and necessary for the analysis."),

    # Statistical Tests:
    # The statistical tests used in the analysis are appropriate for the data and the research questions.
    "Statistical tests": ("OK", "The statistical tests used in the analysis are appropriate for the data and the research questions.")
}

[12] ==== SURROGATE {Performer} -> "Data Analysis Code"  =============================================================================================
Here is the code to perform the analysis.
It creates the files: ['table_0.pkl', 'table_1.pkl', 'table_2.pkl', 'additional_results.pkl'].
# IMPORT
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import pickle
from statsmodels.formula.api import ols
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler

# LOAD DATA
data_infection = pd.read_csv('TimeToInfection.csv')
data_symptoms = pd.read_csv('Symptoms.csv')

# DATASET PREPARATIONS
# Merge the two dataframes based on 'ID' and 'group'
data_all = pd.merge(data_infection, data_symptoms, on=['ID', 'group'])
data_infected = data_all[data_all['infection_event'] == 1].drop_duplicates(subset='ID')

# Convert 'positive_household' to binary
data_infected['positive_household_binary'] = data_infected['positive_household'].map({'yes': 1, 'no': 0})

# Exclude unimmunised group
data_infected_immunised = data_infected[data_infected['group'] != 'N']

# DESCRIPTIVE STATISTICS
## Table 0: "Descriptive statistics of infection events, mean symptoms & mean time since last infection per group"
summary_infection_events = data_all.groupby('group')['infection_event'].sum()
summary_avg_symptoms = data_infected.groupby('group')['symptom_number'].mean()
summary_avg_time_since_immunisation = data_infected.groupby('group')['months_since_immunisation'].mean()

df0 = pd.DataFrame({'InfectionEvents': summary_infection_events,
                    'MeanSymptoms': summary_avg_symptoms,
                    'MeanMonthsSinceImmunisation': summary_avg_time_since_immunisation})

# Replacing NaN values with "NA" for group N
df0.fillna("NA", inplace=True)
df0.to_pickle('table_0.pkl')

# PREPROCESSING
# Data Standardization
scaler = StandardScaler()
scaled_data = scaler.fit_transform(data_infected_immunised[['age_x', 'months_since_immunisation']])
scaled_df = pd.DataFrame(scaled_data, index=data_infected_immunised.index, columns=['scaled_age',
                                                                                   'scaled_months_since_immunisation'])
data_infected_immunised = pd.concat([data_infected_immunised, scaled_df], axis=1)

# Creating dummy variables for categorical variables
data_infected_immunised = pd.get_dummies(data_infected_immunised, drop_first=True)

# ANALYSIS
## Table 1: "Association between months_since_immunisation and symptom_number, including interaction between group and time since immunisation"
model1 = ols('symptom_number ~ scaled_months_since_immunisation * C(group_V + group_I)',
              data=data_infected_immunised).fit()
table1 = model1.summary2().tables[1]
table1.to_pickle('table_1.pkl')

## Table 2: "Association between patient_contact & symptom_number, adjusted for mask usage & household infections"
model2 = ols('symptom_number ~ patient_contact + using_FFP2_mask_x + positive_household_binary',
              data=data_infected_immunised).fit()
table2 = model2.summary2().tables[1]
table2.to_pickle('table_2.pkl')

# SAVE ADDITIONAL RESULTS
additional_results = {
    'Total number of observations': len(data_all),
    'Number of immunised and infected health workers': len(data_infected_immunised),
}

with open('additional_results.pkl', 'wb') as f:
    pickle.dump(additional_results, f)
======================================================================================================================================================

[12] ==== SURROGATE {Performer} -> "Data Analysis Code"  =============================================================================================
Here is the code to perform the analysis.
It creates the files: ['table_0.pkl', 'table_1.pkl', 'table_2.pkl', 'additional_results.pkl'].
# IMPORT
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import pickle
from statsmodels.formula.api import ols
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler

# LOAD DATA
data_infection = pd.read_csv('TimeToInfection.csv')
data_symptoms = pd.read_csv('Symptoms.csv')

# DATASET PREPARATIONS
# Merge the two dataframes based on 'ID' and 'group'
data_all = pd.merge(data_infection, data_symptoms, on=['ID', 'group'])
data_infected = data_all[data_all['infection_event'] == 1].drop_duplicates(subset='ID')

# Convert 'positive_household' to binary
data_infected['positive_household_binary'] = data_infected['positive_household'].map({'yes': 1, 'no': 0})

# Exclude unimmunised group
data_infected_immunised = data_infected[data_infected['group'] != 'N']

# DESCRIPTIVE STATISTICS
## Table 0: "Descriptive statistics of infection events, mean symptoms & mean time since last infection per group"
summary_infection_events = data_all.groupby('group')['infection_event'].sum()
summary_avg_symptoms = data_infected.groupby('group')['symptom_number'].mean()
summary_avg_time_since_immunisation = data_infected.groupby('group')['months_since_immunisation'].mean()

df0 = pd.DataFrame({'InfectionEvents': summary_infection_events,
                    'MeanSymptoms': summary_avg_symptoms,
                    'MeanMonthsSinceImmunisation': summary_avg_time_since_immunisation})

# Replacing NaN values with "NA" for group N
df0.fillna("NA", inplace=True)
df0.to_pickle('table_0.pkl')

# PREPROCESSING
# Data Standardization
scaler = StandardScaler()
scaled_data = scaler.fit_transform(data_infected_immunised[['age_x', 'months_since_immunisation']])
scaled_df = pd.DataFrame(scaled_data, index=data_infected_immunised.index, columns=['scaled_age',
                                                                                   'scaled_months_since_immunisation'])
data_infected_immunised = pd.concat([data_infected_immunised, scaled_df], axis=1)

# Creating dummy variables for categorical variables
data_infected_immunised = pd.get_dummies(data_infected_immunised, drop_first=True)

# Creating interaction terms
data_infected_immunised['group_V_months_since_immunisation'] = data_infected_immunised['group_V'] *
data_infected_immunised['scaled_months_since_immunisation']
data_infected_immunised['group_I_months_since_immunisation'] = data_infected_immunised['group_I'] *
data_infected_immunised['scaled_months_since_immunisation']

# ANALYSIS
## Table 1: "Association between months_since_immunisation and symptom_number, including interaction between group and time since immunisation"
model1 = ols('symptom_number ~ scaled_months_since_immunisation + group_V_months_since_immunisation + group_I_months_since_immunisation',
              data=data_infected_immunised).fit()
table1 = model1.summary2().tables[1]
table1.to_pickle('table_1.pkl')

## Table 2: "Association between patient_contact & symptom_number, adjusted for mask usage & household infections"
model2 = ols('symptom_number ~ patient_contact + using_FFP2_mask_x + positive_household_binary',
              data=data_infected_immunised).fit()
table2 = model2.summary2().tables[1]
table2.to_pickle('table_2.pkl')

# SAVE ADDITIONAL RESULTS
additional_results = {
    'Total number of observations': len(data_all),
    'Number of immunised and infected health workers': len(data_infected_immunised),
}

with open('additional_results.pkl', 'wb') as f:
    pickle.dump(additional_results, f)
======================================================================================================================================================

AppendMessage, Adding the debugged code as if it was the original response.
[12] ==== SURROGATE {Performer} -> "Data Analysis Code"  =============================================================================================
Here is the code to perform the analysis.
It creates the files: ['table_0.pkl', 'table_1.pkl', 'table_2.pkl', 'additional_results.pkl'].
# IMPORT
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import pickle
from statsmodels.formula.api import ols
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler

# LOAD DATA
data_infection = pd.read_csv('TimeToInfection.csv')
data_symptoms = pd.read_csv('Symptoms.csv')

# DATASET PREPARATIONS
# Merge the two dataframes based on 'ID' and 'group'
data_all = pd.merge(data_infection, data_symptoms, on=['ID', 'group'])
data_infected = data_all[data_all['infection_event'] == 1].drop_duplicates(subset='ID')

# Convert 'positive_household' to binary
data_infected['positive_household_binary'] = data_infected['positive_household'].map({'yes': 1, 'no': 0})

# Exclude unimmunised group
data_infected_immunised = data_infected[data_infected['group'] != 'N']

# DESCRIPTIVE STATISTICS
## Table 0: "Descriptive statistics of infection events, mean symptoms & mean time since last infection per group"
summary_infection_events = data_all.groupby('group')['infection_event'].sum()
summary_avg_symptoms = data_infected.groupby('group')['symptom_number'].mean()
summary_avg_time_since_immunisation = data_infected.groupby('group')['months_since_immunisation'].mean()

df0 = pd.DataFrame({'InfectionEvents': summary_infection_events,
                    'MeanSymptoms': summary_avg_symptoms,
                    'MeanMonthsSinceImmunisation': summary_avg_time_since_immunisation})

# Replacing NaN values with "NA" for group N
df0.fillna("NA", inplace=True)
df0.to_pickle('table_0.pkl')

# PREPROCESSING
# Data Standardization
scaler = StandardScaler()
scaled_data = scaler.fit_transform(data_infected_immunised[['age_x', 'months_since_immunisation']])
scaled_df = pd.DataFrame(scaled_data, index=data_infected_immunised.index, columns=['scaled_age', 'scaled_months_since_immunisation'])
data_infected_immunised = pd.concat([data_infected_immunised, scaled_df], axis=1)

# Creating dummy variables for categorical variables
data_infected_immunised = pd.get_dummies(data_infected_immunised, drop_first=True)

# Creating interaction terms
data_infected_immunised['group_V_interaction'] = data_infected_immunised['group_V'] * data_infected_immunised['scaled_months_since_immunisation']
data_infected_immunised['group_I_interaction'] = data_infected_immunised['group_I'] * data_infected_immunised['scaled_months_since_immunisation']

# ANALYSIS
## Table 1: "Association between months_since_immunisation and symptom_number, including interaction between group and time since immunisation"
model1 = ols('symptom_number ~ scaled_months_since_immunisation + group_V + group_I + group_V_interaction + group_I_interaction',
             data=data_infected_immunised).fit()
table1 = model1.summary2().tables[1]
table1.to_pickle('table_1.pkl')

## Table 2: "Association between patient_contact & symptom_number, adjusted for mask usage & household infections"
model2 = ols('symptom_number ~ patient_contact + using_FFP2_mask_x + positive_household_binary',
             data=data_infected_immunised).fit()
table2 = model2.summary2().tables[1]
table2.to_pickle('table_2.pkl')

# SAVE ADDITIONAL RESULTS
additional_results = {
    'Total number of observations': len(data_all),
    'Number of immunised and infected health workers': len(data_infected_immunised),
}

with open('additional_results.pkl', 'wb') as f:
    pickle.dump(additional_results, f)
======================================================================================================================================================

# Data Analysis Code
Here is our Data Analysis Code:
# IMPORT
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import pickle
from statsmodels.formula.api import ols
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler

# LOAD DATA
data_infection = pd.read_csv('TimeToInfection.csv')
data_symptoms = pd.read_csv('Symptoms.csv')

# DATASET PREPARATIONS
# Merge the two dataframes based on 'ID' and 'group'
data_all = pd.merge(data_infection, data_symptoms, on=['ID', 'group'])
data_infected = data_all[data_all['infection_event'] == 1].drop_duplicates(subset='ID')

# Convert 'positive_household' to binary
data_infected['positive_household_binary'] = data_infected['positive_household'].map({'yes': 1, 'no': 0})

# Exclude unimmunised group
data_infected_immunised = data_infected[data_infected['group'] != 'N']

# DESCRIPTIVE STATISTICS
## Table 0: "Descriptive statistics of infection events, mean symptoms & mean time since last infection per group"
summary_infection_events = data_all.groupby('group')['infection_event'].sum()
summary_avg_symptoms = data_infected.groupby('group')['symptom_number'].mean()
summary_avg_time_since_immunisation = data_infected.groupby('group')['months_since_immunisation'].mean()

df0 = pd.DataFrame({'InfectionEvents': summary_infection_events,
                    'MeanSymptoms': summary_avg_symptoms,
                    'MeanMonthsSinceImmunisation': summary_avg_time_since_immunisation})

# Replacing NaN values with "NA" for group N
df0.fillna("NA", inplace=True)
df0.to_pickle('table_0.pkl')

# PREPROCESSING
# Data Standardization
scaler = StandardScaler()
scaled_data = scaler.fit_transform(data_infected_immunised[['age_x', 'months_since_immunisation']])
scaled_df = pd.DataFrame(scaled_data, index=data_infected_immunised.index, columns=['scaled_age', 'scaled_months_since_immunisation'])
data_infected_immunised = pd.concat([data_infected_immunised, scaled_df], axis=1)

# Creating dummy variables for categorical variables
data_infected_immunised = pd.get_dummies(data_infected_immunised, drop_first=True)

# Creating interaction terms
data_infected_immunised['group_V_interaction'] = data_infected_immunised['group_V'] * data_infected_immunised['scaled_months_since_immunisation']
data_infected_immunised['group_I_interaction'] = data_infected_immunised['group_I'] * data_infected_immunised['scaled_months_since_immunisation']

# ANALYSIS
## Table 1: "Association between months_since_immunisation and symptom_number, including interaction between group and time since immunisation"
model1 = ols('symptom_number ~ scaled_months_since_immunisation + group_V + group_I + group_V_interaction + group_I_interaction',
             data=data_infected_immunised).fit()
table1 = model1.summary2().tables[1]
table1.to_pickle('table_1.pkl')

## Table 2: "Association between patient_contact & symptom_number, adjusted for mask usage & household infections"
model2 = ols('symptom_number ~ patient_contact + using_FFP2_mask_x + positive_household_binary',
             data=data_infected_immunised).fit()
table2 = model2.summary2().tables[1]
table2.to_pickle('table_2.pkl')

# SAVE ADDITIONAL RESULTS
additional_results = {
    'Total number of observations': len(data_all),
    'Number of immunised and infected health workers': len(data_infected_immunised),
}

with open('additional_results.pkl', 'wb') as f:
    pickle.dump(additional_results, f)

```latex
\section{Code Explanation}
The provided code conducts data analysis on a dataset consisting of information about healthcare workers in Switzerland, focusing on their infection
events and symptoms. The main steps include:
1. Merging the datasets of infection events and symptoms based on ID and group.
2. Calculating summary statistics such as the number of infection events, mean symptoms, and mean time since last immunisation per group.
3. Preprocessing the data by standardizing numerical variables and creating dummy variables for categorical variables.
4. Creating interaction terms between group type (Vaccination status) and time since immunisation.
5. Performing statistical analysis using linear regression models to assess the associations between variables:
   a. Investigating the association between time since immunisation, group type, and symptoms, including interaction effects.
   b. Assessing the association between patient contact, mask usage, positive household infections, and symptoms.
6. Saving the results of the analysis, including summary tables with regression coefficients and additional results such as the total number of
observations and the number of immunised and infected health workers.

The "additional_results.pkl" file contains a dictionary with two key-value pairs storing the total number of observations in the dataset and the count
of immunised and infected health workers.
```

[10] COMMENTER -> "LaTeX Table Design Code"  <after_background>  : Background messages completed. Requesting "LaTeX Table Design code".

[11] ---- USER {InterpretationReviewer} -> "LaTeX Table Design Code"  --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Please write a Python code to convert and re-style the "table_?.pkl" dataframes created by our "Data Analysis Code" into latex tables suitable for our
scientific paper.

Your code should use the following 3 custom functions provided for import from `my_utils`:

def to_latex_with_note(df, filename: str, caption: str, label: str, note: str = None, legend: Dict[str, str] = None, **kwargs):
    """
    Converts a DataFrame to a LaTeX table with optional note and legend added below the table.

    Parameters:
    - df, filename, caption, label: as in `df.to_latex`.
    - note (optional): Additional note below the table.
    - legend (optional): Dictionary mapping abbreviations to full names.
    - **kwargs: Additional arguments for `df.to_latex`.
    """

def is_str_in_df(df: pd.DataFrame, s: str):
    return any(s in level for level in getattr(df.index, 'levels', [df.index]) + getattr(df.columns, 'levels', [df.columns]))

AbbrToNameDef = Dict[Any, Tuple[Optional[str], Optional[str]]]

def split_mapping(abbrs_to_names_and_definitions: AbbrToNameDef):
    abbrs_to_names = {abbr: name for abbr, (name, definition) in abbrs_to_names_and_definitions.items() if name is not None}
    names_to_definitions = {name or abbr: definition for abbr, (name, definition) in abbrs_to_names_and_definitions.items() if definition is not None}
    return abbrs_to_names, names_to_definitions


Your code should:

* Rename column and row names: You should provide a new name to any column or row label that is abbreviated or technical, or that is otherwise not
self-explanatory.

* Provide legend definitions: You should provide a full definition for any name (or new name) that satisfies any of the following:
- Remains abbreviated, or not self-explanatory, even after renaming.
- Is an ordinal/categorical variable that requires clarification of the meaning of each of its possible values.
- Contains unclear notation, like '*' or ':'
- Represents a numeric variable that has units, that need to be specified.

To avoid re-naming mistakes, you should define for each table a dictionary, `mapping: AbbrToNameDef`, which maps any original column and row names
that are abbreviated or not self-explanatory to an optional new name, and an optional definition.
If different tables share several common labels, then you can build a `shared_mapping`, from which you can extract the relevant labels for each table.

Overall, the code must have the following structure:

# IMPORT
import pandas as pd
from my_utils import to_latex_with_note, is_str_in_df, split_mapping, AbbrToNameDef

# PREPARATION FOR ALL TABLES
# <As applicable, define a shared mapping for labels that are common to all tables. For example:>
shared_mapping: AbbrToNameDef = {
    'AvgAge': ('Avg. Age', 'Average age, years'),
    'BT': ('Body Temperature', '1: Normal, 2: High, 3: Very High'),
    'W': ('Weight', 'Participant weight, kg'),
    'MRSA': (None, 'Infected with Methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus, 1: Yes, 0: No'),
    ...: (..., ...),
}
# <This is of course just an example. Consult with the "Description of the Original Dataset" and the "Data Analysis Code" for choosing the labels and
their proper scientific names and definitions.>

# TABLE 0:
df0 = pd.read_pickle('table_0.pkl')

# FORMAT VALUES <include this sub-section only as applicable>
# <Rename technical values to scientifically-suitable values. For example:>
df0['MRSA'] = df0['MRSA'].apply(lambda x: 'Yes' if x == 1 else 'No')

# RENAME ROWS AND COLUMNS <include this sub-section only as applicable>
# <Rename any abbreviated or not self-explanatory table labels to scientifically-suitable names.>
# <Use the `shared_mapping` if applicable. For example:>
mapping0 = dict((k, v) for k, v in shared_mapping.items() if is_str_in_df(df0, k))
mapping0 |= {
    'PV': ('P-value', None),
    'CI': (None, '95% Confidence Interval'),
    'Sex_Age': ('Age * Sex', 'Interaction term between Age and Sex'),
}
abbrs_to_names0, legend0 = split_mapping(mapping0)
df0 = df0.rename(columns=abbrs_to_names0, index=abbrs_to_names0)

# SAVE AS LATEX:
to_latex_with_note(
    df0, 'table_0.tex',
    caption="<choose a caption suitable for a table in a scientific paper>",
    label='table:<chosen table label>',
    note="<If needed, add a note to provide any additional information that is not captured in the caption>",
    legend=legend0)
# TABLE <?>:
# <etc, all 'table_?.pkl' files>


Avoid the following:
Do not provide a sketch or pseudocode; write a complete runnable code including all '# HEADERS' sections.
Do not create any graphics, figures or any plots.
Do not send any presumed output examples.

# IMPORT
import pandas as pd
from my_utils import to_latex_with_note, is_str_in_df, split_mapping, AbbrToNameDef
from typing import Optional, Dict, Any, Tuple

# Defining shared mappings
shared_mapping: AbbrToNameDef = {
    'Coef.': ('Coefficient', None),
    'Std.Err.': ('Standard Error', None),
    'P>|t|': ('Probability Value', None),
    '[0.025': ('Confidence Interval Start', None),
    '0.975]': ('Confidence Interval End', None),
    'MeanSymptoms': ('Mean Symptom Count', 'Average number of symptoms reported by each group'),
    'mean_months_since_immunisation': ('Mean Months Since Immunisation', 'Average duration (in months) since the last immunisation event'),
    'InfectionEvents': ('Total Infection Events', 'Number of infection events reported by each group'),
}

# Loading and transforming Table 0
df0 = pd.read_pickle('table_0.pkl')

# Defining specific mapping for df0
mapping0 = dict((k, v) for k, v in shared_mapping.items() if is_str_in_df(df0, k))
mapping0 |= {
    'group': ('Vaccination/Infection Group', '''"N" (no immunity), "V" (twice vaccinated, uninfected),
    "I" (infected, unvaccinated), "H" (hybrid: infected and ≥1 vaccination)'''),
}

# Splitting mapping into names and legend
abbrs_to_names0, legend0 = split_mapping(mapping0)

# Rename column and/or row labels as per mapping
df0 = df0.rename(columns=abbrs_to_names0, index=abbrs_to_names0)

# Export to Latex
to_latex_with_note(
    df0, 'table_0.tex',
    caption="Summary statistics by Vaccination/Infection group",
    label='table:summary_statistics',
    note="",
    legend=legend0)

# Loading and transforming Table 1
df1 = pd.read_pickle('table_1.pkl')

# Defining specific mapping for df1
mapping1 = dict((k, v) for k, v in shared_mapping.items() if is_str_in_df(df1, k))
mapping1 |= {
    'scaled_months_since_immunisation': ('Scaled Time Since Immunization', None),
    'group_V[T.True]': ('Vaccinated Group', None),
    'group_I[T.True]': ('Infected Group', None),
    'group_V_interaction': ('Interaction: Vaccinated & Time', 'Interaction term between Vaccine Group and Time Since Immunization'),
    'group_I_interaction': ('Interaction: Infected & Time', 'Interaction term between Infected Group and Time Since Immunization'),
}

# Splitting mapping into names and legend
abbrs_to_names1, legend1 = split_mapping(mapping1)

# Rename column and/or row labels as per mapping
df1 = df1.rename(columns=abbrs_to_names1, index=abbrs_to_names1)

# Export to Latex
to_latex_with_note(
    df1, 'table_1.tex',
    caption="Association between time since immunisations and symptom numbers",
    label='table:time_immunisation',
    note="P-values < 0.05 are considered statistically significant",
    legend=legend1)

# Loading and transforming Table 2
df2 = pd.read_pickle('table_2.pkl')

# Defining specific mapping for df2
mapping2 = dict((k, v) for k, v in shared_mapping.items() if is_str_in_df(df2, k))
mapping2 |= {
    'patient_contact': ('Patient Contact', '1 if healthcare worker has contact with patients, 0 otherwise'),
    'using_FFP2_mask_x': ('Using FFP2 Mask', '1 if healthcare worker uses FFP2 protective respiratory masks, 0 otherwise'),
    'positive_household_binary': ('Positive Household Contact', '1 if a household contact was positive for SARS-CoV-2 within same month, 0
otherwise'),
}

# Splitting mapping into names and legend
abbrs_to_names2, legend2 = split_mapping(mapping2)

# Rename column and/or row labels as per mapping
df2 = df2.rename(columns=abbrs_to_names2, index=abbrs_to_names2)

# Export to Latex
to_latex_with_note(
    df2, 'table_2.tex',
    caption="Association between patient contact and symptom number",
    label='table:patient_contact_symptoms',
    note="Data adjusted for mask usage and household infections",
    legend=legend2)

import pandas as pd
from my_utils import to_latex_with_note, is_str_in_df, split_mapping, AbbrToNameDef
from typing import Optional, Dict, Any, Tuple

# Defining shared mappings
shared_mapping: AbbrToNameDef = {
    'Coef.': ('Coefficient', None),
    'Std.Err.': ('Standard Error', None),
    'P>|t|': ('Probability Value', None),
    '[0.025': ('Confidence Interval Start', None),
    '0.975]': ('Confidence Interval End', None),
    'MeanSymptoms': ('Mean Symptom Count', 'Average number of symptoms reported by each group'),
    'mean_months_since_immunisation': ('Mean Months Since Immunisation', 'Average duration (in months) since the last immunisation event'),
    'InfectionEvents': ('Total Infection Events', 'Number of infection events reported by each group'),
}

mapping0 |= {
    'group': ('Vaccination/Infection Group', '''"N" (no immunity), "V" (twice vaccinated, uninfected),
    "I" (infected, unvaccinated), "H" (hybrid: infected and ≥1 vaccination)'''),
}

# Splitting mapping into names and legend
abbrs_to_names0, legend0 = split_mapping(mapping0)

# Rename column and/or row labels as per mapping
df0 = df0.rename(columns=abbrs_to_names0, index=abbrs_to_names0)

# Export to Latex
to_latex_with_note(
    df0, 'table_0.tex',
    caption="Summary statistics by Vaccination/Infection group",
    label='table:summary_statistics',
    note="",
    legend=legend0)

# Defining specific mapping for df1
mapping1 = dict((k, v) for k, v in shared_mapping.items() if is_str_in_df(df1, k))

    'group_V[T.True]': ('Vaccinated Group', None),
    'group_I[T.True]': ('Infected Group', None),
    'group_V_interaction': ('Interaction: Vaccinated & Time', 'Interaction term between Vaccine Group and Time Since Immunization'),

}

    df1, 'table_1.tex',
    caption="Association between time since immunisations and symptom numbers",
    label='table:time_immunisation',
    note="P-values < 0.05 are considered statistically significant",

# Defining specific mapping for df2

    'positive_household_binary': ('Positive Household Contact', '1 if a household contact was positive for SARS-CoV-2 within same month, 0

}

# Rename column and/or row labels as per mapping

    df2, 'table_2.tex',

# IMPORT
import pandas as pd
from my_utils import to_latex_with_note, is_str_in_df, split_mapping, AbbrToNameDef
from typing import Optional, Dict, Any, Tuple

# PREPARATION FOR ALL TABLES
shared_mapping: AbbrToNameDef = {
    'Coef.': ('Coefficient', None),
    'Std.Err.': ('Standard Error', None),
    'P>|t|': ('Probability Value', None),
    '[0.025': ('Confidence Interval Start', None),
    '0.975]': ('Confidence Interval End', None),
    'MeanSymptoms': ('Mean Symptom Count', 'Average number of symptoms reported by each group'),
    'MeanMonthsSinceImmunisation': ('Mean Months Since Immunisation', 'Average duration (in months) since the last immunisation event'),
    'InfectionEvents': ('Total Infection Events', 'Number of infection events reported by each group'),
}

# TABLE 0:
df0 = pd.read_pickle('table_0.pkl')
mapping0 = {
    k: v for k, v in shared_mapping.items() if is_str_in_df(df0, k)
}
mapping0 |= {
    'group': ('Vaccination/Infection Group', '''"N" (no immunity), "V" (twice vaccinated, uninfected),
    "I" (infected, unvaccinated), "H" (hybrid: infected and ≥1 vaccination)'''),
}
abbrs_to_names0, legend0 = split_mapping(mapping0)
df0.rename(columns=abbrs_to_names0, index=abbrs_to_names0, inplace=True)
to_latex_with_note(df0, 'table_0.tex',
                   caption="Summary statistics by Vaccination/Infection group",
                   label='table:summary_statistics',
                   note="",
                   legend=legend0)

# TABLE 1:
df1 = pd.read_pickle('table_1.pkl')
mapping1 = {
    k: v for k, v in shared_mapping.items() if is_str_in_df(df1, k)
}
mapping1 |= {
    'scaled_months_since_immunisation': ('Scaled Time Since Immunization', None),
    'group_V[T.True]': ('Vaccinated Group', None),
    'group_I[T.True]': ('Infected Group', None),
    'group_V_interaction': ('Interaction: Vaccinated & Time', 'Interaction term between Vaccine Group and Time Since Immunization'),
    'group_I_interaction': ('Interaction: Infected & Time', 'Interaction term between Infected Group and Time Since Immunization'),
}
abbrs_to_names1, legend1 = split_mapping(mapping1)
df1.rename(columns=abbrs_to_names1, index=abbrs_to_names1, inplace=True)
to_latex_with_note(
    df1, 'table_1.tex',
    caption="Association between time since immunisations and symptom numbers",
    label='table:time_immunisation',
    note="P-values < 0.05 are considered statistically significant",
    legend=legend1)

# TABLE 2:
df2 = pd.read_pickle('table_2.pkl')
mapping2 = {
    k: v for k, v in shared_mapping.items() if is_str_in_df(df2, k)
}
mapping2 |= {
    'patient_contact': ('Patient Contact', '1 if healthcare worker has contact with patients, 0 otherwise'),
    'using_FFP2_mask_x': ('Using FFP2 Mask', '1 if healthcare worker uses FFP2 protective respiratory masks, 0 otherwise'),
    'positive_household_binary': ('Positive Household Contact', '1 if a household contact was positive for SARS-CoV-2 within same month, 0
otherwise'),
}
abbrs_to_names2, legend2 = split_mapping(mapping2)
df2.rename(columns=abbrs_to_names2, index=abbrs_to_names2, inplace=True)
to_latex_with_note(
    df2, 'table_2.tex',
    caption="Association between patient contact and symptom number",
    label='table:patient_contact_symptoms',
    note="Data adjusted for mask usage and household infections",
    legend=legend2)

Here is the code to perform the requested analysis:
# IMPORT
import pandas as pd
from my_utils import to_latex_with_note, is_str_in_df, split_mapping, AbbrToNameDef
from typing import Optional, Dict, Any, Tuple

# PREPARATION FOR ALL TABLES
shared_mapping: AbbrToNameDef = {
    'Coef.': ('Coefficient', None),
    'Std.Err.': ('Standard Error', None),
    'P>|t|': ('Probability Value', None),
    '[0.025': ('Confidence Interval Start', None),
    '0.975]': ('Confidence Interval End', None),
    'MeanSymptoms': ('Mean Symptom Count', 'Average number of symptoms reported by each group'),
    'MeanMonthsSinceImmunisation': ('Mean Months Since Immunisation', 'Average duration (in months) since the last immunisation event'),
    'InfectionEvents': ('Total Infection Events', 'Number of infection events reported by each group'),
}

# TABLE 0:
df0 = pd.read_pickle('table_0.pkl')
mapping0 = {
    k: v for k, v in shared_mapping.items() if is_str_in_df(df0, k)
}
mapping0 |= {
    'group': ('Vaccination/Infection Group', '''"N" (no immunity), "V" (twice vaccinated, uninfected),
    "I" (infected, unvaccinated), "H" (hybrid: infected and ≥1 vaccination)'''),
}
abbrs_to_names0, legend0 = split_mapping(mapping0)
df0.rename(columns=abbrs_to_names0, index=abbrs_to_names0, inplace=True)
to_latex_with_note(df0, 'table_0.tex',
                   caption="Summary statistics by Vaccination/Infection group",
                   label='table:summary_statistics',
                   note="",
                   legend=legend0)

# TABLE 1:
df1 = pd.read_pickle('table_1.pkl')
mapping1 = {
    k: v for k, v in shared_mapping.items() if is_str_in_df(df1, k)
}
mapping1 |= {
    'scaled_months_since_immunisation': ('Scaled Time Since Immunization', None),
    'group_V[T.True]': ('Vaccinated Group', None),
    'group_I[T.True]': ('Infected Group', None),
    'group_V_interaction': ('Interaction: Vaccinated & Time', 'Interaction term between Vaccine Group and Time Since Immunization'),
    'group_I_interaction': ('Interaction: Infected & Time', 'Interaction term between Infected Group and Time Since Immunization'),
}
abbrs_to_names1, legend1 = split_mapping(mapping1)
df1.rename(columns=abbrs_to_names1, index=abbrs_to_names1, inplace=True)
to_latex_with_note(
    df1, 'table_1.tex',
    caption="Association between time since immunisations and symptom numbers",
    label='table:time_immunisation',
    note="P-values < 0.05 are considered statistically significant",
    legend=legend1)

# TABLE 2:
df2 = pd.read_pickle('table_2.pkl')
mapping2 = {
    k: v for k, v in shared_mapping.items() if is_str_in_df(df2, k)
}
mapping2 |= {
    'patient_contact': ('Patient Contact', '1 if healthcare worker has contact with patients, 0 otherwise'),
    'using_FFP2_mask_x': ('Using FFP2 Mask', '1 if healthcare worker uses FFP2 protective respiratory masks, 0 otherwise'),
    'positive_household_binary': ('Positive Household Contact', '1 if a household contact was positive for SARS-CoV-2 within same month, 0
otherwise'),
}
abbrs_to_names2, legend2 = split_mapping(mapping2)
df2.rename(columns=abbrs_to_names2, index=abbrs_to_names2, inplace=True)
to_latex_with_note(
    df2, 'table_2.tex',
    caption="Association between patient contact and symptom number",
    label='table:patient_contact_symptoms',
    note="Data adjusted for mask usage and household infections",
    legend=legend2)

# Table too wide
## table_1.tex:
Here is the created table:

```latex
\begin{table}[h]
\caption{Association between time since immunisations and symptom numbers}
\label{table:time_immunisation}
\begin{threeparttable}
\renewcommand{\TPTminimum}{\linewidth}
\makebox[\linewidth]{%
\begin{tabular}{lrrrlrr}
\toprule
 & Coefficient & Standard Error & t & Probability Value & Confidence Interval Start & Confidence Interval End \\
\midrule
\textbf{Intercept} & 2.99 & 0.221 & 13.6 & $<$1e-06 & 2.56 & 3.43 \\
\textbf{Vaccinated Group} & 0.838 & 0.239 & 3.51 & 0.00048 & 0.369 & 1.31 \\
\textbf{Infected Group} & 1.93 & 0.644 & 2.99 & 0.00291 & 0.66 & 3.19 \\
\textbf{Scaled Time Since Immunization} & -0.273 & 0.201 & -1.36 & 0.174 & -0.668 & 0.121 \\
\textbf{Interaction: Vaccinated \& Time} & 0.174 & 0.225 & 0.772 & 0.441 & -0.268 & 0.615 \\
\textbf{Interaction: Infected \& Time} & -0.194 & 0.354 & -0.548 & 0.584 & -0.89 & 0.502 \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}}
\begin{tablenotes}
\footnotesize
\item P-values $<$ 0.05 are considered statistically significant
\item \textbf{Interaction: Vaccinated \& Time}: Interaction term between Vaccine Group and Time Since Immunization
\item \textbf{Interaction: Infected \& Time}: Interaction term between Infected Group and Time Since Immunization
\end{tablenotes}
\end{threeparttable}
\end{table}
```
I tried to compile it, but the table is too wide.
Please change the code to make the table narrower. Consider any of the following options:

- Rename column labels to shorter names. Use `df.rename(columns=...)`

- Rename the index labels to shorter names. Use `df.rename(index=...)`
- Drop unnecessary columns. If the index labels cannot be shortened much, consider whether there might be any unnecessary columns that we can drop.
Use `to_latex_with_note(df, filename, columns=...)`.

[13] ==== SURROGATE {Performer} -> "LaTeX Table Design Code"  ========================================================================================
Here is the code to perform the requested analysis:
# IMPORT
import pandas as pd
from my_utils import to_latex_with_note, is_str_in_df, split_mapping, AbbrToNameDef
from typing import Optional, Dict, Any, Tuple

# PREPARATION FOR ALL TABLES
shared_mapping: AbbrToNameDef = {
    'Coef.': ('Coeff.', None),
    'Std.Err.': ('Std. Err.', None),
    'P>|t|': ('p-value', None),
    '[0.025': ('CI Start', None),
    '0.975]': ('CI End', None),
    'MeanSymptoms': ('Mean Symptom Count', 'Average number of symptoms reported by each group'),
    'MeanMonthsSinceImmunisation': ('Mean Mos. Since Immun.', 'Average duration (in months) since the last immunisation event'),
    'InfectionEvents': ('Infection Events', 'Number of infection events reported by each group'),
}

# TABLE 0:
df0 = pd.read_pickle('table_0.pkl')
mapping0 = {
    k: v for k, v in shared_mapping.items() if is_str_in_df(df0, k)
}
mapping0 |= {
    'group': ('Immune Group', '''"N" (no immunity), "V" (twice vaccinated, uninfected),
    "I" (infected, unvaccinated), "H" (hybrid: infected and ≥1 vaccination)'''),
}
abbrs_to_names0, legend0 = split_mapping(mapping0)
df0.rename(columns=abbrs_to_names0, index=abbrs_to_names0, inplace=True)
to_latex_with_note(df0, 'table_0.tex',
                   caption="Summary statistics by Immune group",
                   label='table:summary_statistics',
                   note="",
                   legend=legend0)

# TABLE 1:
df1 = pd.read_pickle('table_1.pkl')
mapping1 = {
    k: v for k, v in shared_mapping.items() if is_str_in_df(df1, k)
}
mapping1 |= {
    'scaled_months_since_immunisation': ('Time Since Immun.', None),
    'group_V[T.True]': ('Vac. Group', None),
    'group_I[T.True]': ('Infected Group', None),
    'group_V_interaction': ('Vac. & Time', 'Interaction: Vaccine Group and Time Since Immunization'),
    'group_I_interaction': ('Inf. & Time', 'Interaction: Infected Group and Time Since Immunization'),
}
abbrs_to_names1, legend1 = split_mapping(mapping1)
df1.rename(columns=abbrs_to_names1, index=abbrs_to_names1, inplace=True)
to_latex_with_note(
    df1, 'table_1.tex',
    caption="Association between time since immunisations and symptom numbers",
    label='table:time_immunisation',
    note="P-values < 0.05 considered statistically significant",
    legend=legend1,
    columns=['Coeff.', 'Std. Err.', 'p-value'])  # This will remove three columns with the least significant meaning

# TABLE 2:
df2 = pd.read_pickle('table_2.pkl')
mapping2 = {
    k: v for k, v in shared_mapping.items() if is_str_in_df(df2, k)
}
mapping2 |= {
    'patient_contact': ('Patient Contact', '1 if healthcare worker has contact with patients'),
    'using_FFP2_mask_x': ('Using FFP2', '1 if healthcare worker uses FFP2 protective respiratory masks'),
    'positive_household_binary': ('Household Contact', '1 if a household contact was positive for SARS-CoV-2 within same month'),
}
abbrs_to_names2, legend2 = split_mapping(mapping2)
df2.rename(columns=abbrs_to_names2, index=abbrs_to_names2, inplace=True)
to_latex_with_note(
    df2, 'table_2.tex',
    caption="Association between patient contact and symptom number",
    label='table:patient_contact_symptoms',
    note="Data adjusted for mask usage and household infections",
    legend=legend2)
